questions.question: What is the topic for Week 36?
questions.explanation: The topic explicitly listed for Lesson Thirty-Six is PARENTING STYLES (PART 2).

questions.question: Which of the following was the topic for Week 35?
questions.explanation: Parenting Styles (Part 1) was the topic for Week 35.

questions.question: He that spareth his rod hateth his son: but he that loveth him chasteneth him ________.
questions.explanation: The memory verse (Proverbs 13:24) says 'but he that loveth him chasteneth him betimes.'

questions.question: Provide the book, chapter, and verse for this week's memory verse.
questions.explanation: The memory verse reference is Proverbs 13:24.

questions.question: Type out the memory verse for Week 36 in full (KJV/NKJV).
questions.answer: He that spareth his rod hateth his son: but he that loveth him chasteneth him betimes.
questions.explanation: Full text recall of Proverbs 13:24.

questions.question: According to the memory verse (Proverbs 13:24), what does a father who spares his rod do?
questions.explanation: The verse says 'He that spareth his rod hateth his son...'

questions.question: According to the introduction, the four major parenting styles are permissive, uninvolved, authoritarian, and authoritative.
questions.explanation: The text states 'In our last lesson, we discussed the four major parenting styles namely the “permissive”, “uninvolved”, “authoritarian” and “authoritative.”'

questions.question: What significant responsibility of Christian parents does the lesson focus on?
options: B. Raising their children in a way that honours God and promotes spiritual growth, emotional well-being and career success
C. Ensuring their children play sports
questions.explanation: The introduction explicitly states this responsibility.

questions.question: What is the heading of the first outline?
questions.explanation: Outline I is titled 'BIBLICAL POINTERS'.

questions.question: According to Outline I, does the Bible have anything to say about different parenting styles?
options: A. No, it is silent on the matter
C. Yes, it gives several examples of very effective techniques and disastrous methods
questions.explanation: The text says 'Yes, it does. The Bible gives several examples of very effective parenting techniques, and some disastrous parenting methods...'

questions.question: Which scripture is cited to show the Bible emphasizes spiritual instruction for parents to follow?
questions.explanation: The text lists 'spiritual instruction (Deuteronomy 6:7; Proverbs 22:6).'

questions.question: According to the lesson, knowing the biblical pointers is enough to make a person a godly and effective parent.
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states 'Knowing these pointers are not enough to make the necessary changes! Obeying them is crucial...'

questions.question: Which scripture is cited to show the Bible provides guidance for parents by emphasizing love, care and support?
questions.explanation: The text lists 'love, care and support (Ephesians 6:4; 1Timothy 5:8; Proverbs 31:15, 21)...'

questions.question: What is the heading of the second outline?
questions.explanation: Outline II is titled 'THE BEST PARENTING STYLE'.

questions.question: According to Outline II, does parenting follow a 'one size fits all' model?
options: A. Yes, all children are the same
B. Yes, authoritarian is the only way
C. No, parenting does not follow a 'one size fits all' model
D. Yes, if you use the Bible
questions.explanation: The text states 'Parenting does not follow a “one size fits all” model.'

questions.question: What do the most effective parents do regarding their parenting style?
options: A. They never change their style
B. They recognise when to adjust their style based on the circumstances
C. They let the child pick the style
D. They let the school teachers handle it
questions.explanation: The text states 'The most effective parents recognise when to adjust their style based on the circumstances.'

questions.question: An authoritarian parent might choose to be more permissive when a child is unwell, offering extra warmth.
questions.explanation: The text gives this as an example: 'For instance, an authoritarian parent might choose to be more permissive when a child is unwell, offering extra warmth and easing some restrictions...'

questions.question: When might a permissive parent adopt a stricter approach?
options: A. When they want to sleep
B. When a child's safety is at risk, such as while crossing a busy street
D. When they don't have enough money
questions.explanation: The text says 'A permissive parent might adopt a stricter approach when a child’s safety is at risk, such as while crossing a busy street...'

questions.question: According to the conclusion, what should guide you in using the parenting style that best suits your family's needs?
options: B. The guidance of the Holy Spirit
questions.explanation: The conclusion states 'Use the parenting style that best suits your family’s needs with the guidance of the Holy Spirit.'

questions.question: Where is this verse found? 'Esther had not shewed her people nor her kindred: for Mordecai had charged her that she should not shew it.'
questions.verse_text: Esther had not shewed her people nor her kindred: for Mordecai had charged her that she should not shew it.
questions.explanation: This is verse 10 from the main Bible Passage.

questions.question: According to the Bible Passage (Esther 2:7), who brought up Hadassah (Esther)?
questions.explanation: Verse 7 says 'And he brought up Hadassah... whom Mordecai, when her father and mother were dead, took for his own daughter.'

questions.question: What is another name for Esther in Esther 2:7?
questions.explanation: Verse 7 says 'Hadassah, that is, Esther...'

questions.question: According to Esther 2:7, what happened to Esther's father and mother?
options: A. They moved to another country
questions.explanation: Verse 7 says 'for she had neither father nor mother... when her father and mother were dead...'

questions.question: In Esther 2:10, why did Esther not show her people nor her kindred?
options: A. She forgot who they were
B. Mordecai had charged her that she should not show it
C. She was ashamed of them
questions.explanation: Verse 10 says 'for Mordecai had charged her that she should not shew it.'

questions.question: According to Esther 2:11, what did Mordecai do every day?
options: B. He walked before the court of the women's house, to know how Esther did
C. He wrote letters to Esther
questions.explanation: Verse 11 says 'And Mordecai walked every day before the court of the women’s house, to know how Esther did, and what should become of her.'

questions.question: Your parenting style simply refers to your ________ to parenting.
questions.explanation: Outline I says 'Your parenting style simply refers to your approach to parenting.'

questions.question: You do not have to stick to a single parenting approach, as there will be times when a mix of methods is necessary.
questions.explanation: Outline II explicitly states this.

questions.question: Which scripture is cited regarding God giving examples of disastrous parenting methods?
questions.explanation: Outline I cites '(Genesis 18:19; 1 Samuel 2:27-30)' when mentioning effective and disastrous methods.

questions.question: Knowing these pointers are not enough to make the necessary changes! ________ them is crucial in becoming a godly and effective parent.
questions.explanation: Outline I says 'Obeying them is crucial in becoming a godly and effective parent.'

questions.question: What is the phrase given as an example of a permissive parent being strict for safety?
options: B. 'Of course, you can have ice cream for lunch.'
C. 'I am holding your hand whether you want it or not.'
questions.explanation: Outline II gives the example: 'e.g., “I am holding your hand whether you want it or not.”'
